research.interface: Snowflake SQL API v2, POST /api/v2/statements + GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle} (GA)

official_sources.title: Snowflake SQL API reference
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/v2/statements and GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle}; requestId/retry/async query parameters; ResultSet (code 090001), QueryStatus (code 333334) and QueryFailureStatus schemas; status codes 200/202/400/401/408/422/429 and 5xx retry-with-backoff guidance

official_sources.title: Submitting a request to execute SQL statements
official_sources.used_for: required Authorization/Content-Type/Accept/User-Agent headers; requestId UUID semantics and retry=true resubmission of the same request so a statement is not executed twice; synchronous submissions are not guaranteed to complete synchronously without async=true

official_sources.title: Handling responses
official_sources.used_for: 202 QueryStatus with statementHandle/statementStatusUrl; polling GET /api/v2/statements/{handle} until 200 or 422; 429 as a transient rate-limit signal during polling

official_sources.title: Getting details about an error
official_sources.used_for: 422 QueryFailureStatus fields (code, sqlState, message, statementHandle) for failed executions, distinguished from 400 malformed-request errors

research.language_rationale: Snowflake's .NET driver targets ADO.NET sessions; our ingestion sidecar needs precise transport-level control over requestId reuse and retry classification, which the documented SQL API v2 REST surface exposes directly. C# with HttpClient and System.Text.Json is the idiomatic zero-extra-dependency stack for it.

verified_facts: Statements are submitted with POST /api/v2/statements; requestId is a UUID query parameter, and resubmitting the SAME requestId with retry=true tells Snowflake the request is a resubmission, so DML is not executed a second time.

Every request carries Authorization: Bearer {token}, Accept: application/json and a real User-Agent; POST bodies add Content-Type: application/json; X-Snowflake-Authorization-Token-Type declares the token kind (KEYPAIR_JWT, OAUTH or PROGRAMMATIC_ACCESS_TOKEN).

A submission without async=true is only nominally synchronous: the server may still answer 202 with a QueryStatus (code 333334) carrying statementHandle and statementStatusUrl, and the client must poll GET /api/v2/statements/{handle} — resubmitting the POST instead would risk duplicate execution.
A 200 response is a ResultSet with code 090001, statementHandle, resultSetMetaData (numRows, rowType) and string-encoded data rows (JSON null for SQL NULL).

429 signals rate limiting (retry later, honoring Retry-After when present) and 5xx server errors are retried with exponential backoff; 400 means the request itself is malformed and must not be retried; 422 means the statement failed and returns a QueryFailureStatus with code, sqlState, message and statementHandle.
The bounded retry budget (4 total attempts), the 1/2/4-second fallback backoff and the poll interval are local policy pinned in docs/contract.json, not vendor mandates.
